quick_facts.label: Role
quick_facts.value: A Nursing Officer is a Group B nursing post in government healthcare systems. In AIIMS NORCET-8, AIIMS described the recruitment as for Nursing Officer posts at Pay Level 7 for AIIMS New Delhi and other participating AIIMS, subject to vacancies.

quick_facts.label: Professional registration
quick_facts.value: The 2025 AIIMS NORCET notice required candidates using either the nursing-degree or GNM route to be registered as a Nurse and Midwife with a State Nursing Council or the Indian Nursing Council.

quick_facts.label: Typical AIIMS entry routes
quick_facts.value: For NORCET-8 (notice dated February 24, 2025), eligibility included an INC/State Nursing Council-recognized B.Sc. Nursing, B.Sc. (Hons.) Nursing, Post-Basic B.Sc. Nursing, or GNM route. The GNM route additionally required two years of post-qualification experience in a hospital with at least 50 beds.

quick_facts.label: Government employers
quick_facts.value: Examples include AIIMS institutions recruited through NORCET and the Employees’ State Insurance Corporation (ESIC), whose 2020 recruitment regulations establish a Nursing Officer cadre.

quick_facts.label: Career hierarchy
quick_facts.value: At AIIMS New Delhi, the published nursing-services hierarchy places Nursing Officer below Senior Nursing Officer, Assistant Nursing Superintendent, Deputy Nursing Superintendent, Nursing Superintendent, and Chief Nursing Officer.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: Pay is employer- and notification-specific. ESIC’s 2020 Nursing Officer recruitment regulations list the post at 7th CPC Pay Level 7 (₹44,900–₹1,42,400); the 2025 AIIMS NORCET notice likewise advertised Nursing Officer posts at Level 7 and stated that usual admissible Central Government allowances apply.

sections.heading: Responsibilities
paragraphs: Government Nursing Officers deliver assigned patients’ total nursing care within the employer’s clinical and supervisory structure. AIIMS New Delhi’s nursing manual states that a Nursing Officer is directly responsible to the Senior Nursing Officer (ward in-charge) for the total nursing care of assigned patients.

The AIIMS role description covers direct patient-care work such as admission and discharge support, hygiene and comfort care, patient and family teaching or counselling, administration of medicines and injections, support for IV infusions and transfusions, observation and recording of vital signs, specimen handling, and assistance with clinical tests and procedures. It also includes shift handover, maintaining a therapeutic ward environment, stock and supply-related tasks, orienting staff or students, and reporting significant incidents. Actual duties, delegation, protocols, and clinical deployment vary by government employer and facility.
bullets: Provide and document direct nursing care, including observation, medication-related care, procedures within assigned responsibilities, and patient education.
Coordinate with doctors, nursing supervisors, allied health staff, patients, and relatives.
Undertake ward-management and safety work, including handovers, equipment and supply readiness, infection-control practices, and escalation of emergencies or incidents.
Support teaching, orientation, and supervision of student nurses or newly posted personnel where assigned.

sections.heading: Entry routes and recruitment
paragraphs: The route is normally: complete a recognized nursing qualification, obtain the required Nurse and Midwife registration, satisfy the particular recruiting body’s age, experience, category, and document requirements, qualify in its competitive recruitment process, and complete verification and appointment formalities. Recruitment is not governed by one universal national notice: candidates must use the advertisement and recruitment rules of the specific central or state employer.

AIIMS NORCET is a prominent central route. Its NORCET-8 notice, dated February 24, 2025, accepted recognized B.Sc. Nursing/B.Sc. (Hons.) Nursing/Post-Basic B.Sc. Nursing qualifications with registration, or recognized GNM with registration plus two years’ post-qualification experience in a hospital of at least 50 beds. This is dated notification-specific context, not a permanent rule for every Nursing Officer vacancy.

ESIC is another central-government example. Its Nursing Officer, Senior Nursing Officer and Assistant Nursing Superintendent Recruitment Regulations, 2020 provide for the Nursing Officer cadre and specify that recruitment conditions are set out in the regulations’ schedule. State government health departments, public medical colleges, municipal bodies, and other public employers can use different titles, examinations, eligibility criteria, and selection processes.
bullets: B.Sc. Nursing pathway: obtain the degree from an INC/State Nursing Council-recognized institution or university and complete the registration required by the recruitment notice.

GNM pathway: for the cited 2025 AIIMS NORCET notice, obtain GNM from a recognized institution or board, hold Nurse and Midwife registration, and meet the stated two-year hospital-experience condition.

Recruitment pathway: apply only through the official portal or notice, then meet examination, merit, document-verification, medical-fitness, reservation, and appointment requirements specified for that recruitment.
Before applying, verify the current advertisement because vacancy numbers, age limits, experience requirements, participating institutions, reservation rules, and timelines can change.

sections.heading: Skills developed and used
paragraphs: The role requires clinically safe, patient-centred nursing practice and disciplined documentation. AIIMS’s nursing manual links effective nursing to assessment, planning and implementation, and evaluation; its Nursing Officer duties also require observation, recording, reporting, patient communication, procedural support, and coordination across the ward team.

Because government hospitals may involve high-acuity services and structured nursing hierarchies, the role also relies on escalation judgement, infection-prevention and emergency readiness, reliable shift handovers, inventory awareness, teamwork, and respectful communication with patients and families.
bullets: Clinical assessment, monitoring, documentation, and timely reporting of changes in patient condition.
Medication- and procedure-related nursing competence within institutional policies and supervision arrangements.
Clear patient and family education, counselling, and interprofessional communication.
Shift handover, ward organisation, safety awareness, infection-control practice, and emergency escalation.
Professional accountability, ethical conduct, confidentiality, and continuous in-service learning.

sections.heading: Workplace and employment context
paragraphs: Nursing Officers work in public-sector healthcare settings such as AIIMS hospitals and ESIC medical institutions. At AIIMS, nursing services are described as an integral hospital function providing comprehensive patient care alongside allied disciplines; the published manual also assigns Nursing Officers duties spanning direct care and ward management.

Work is commonly shift-based and can involve wards, outpatient-linked services, procedure areas, operation theatres, critical-care or emergency-facing environments, and other departments according to posting and institutional need. The specific location, rotations, staffing pattern, transfer rules, and service conditions depend on the appointing government organization and its current policies.
bullets: AIIMS NORCET recruitment may allocate successful candidates against vacancies at participating AIIMS and other listed participating institutions or bodies.
ESIC’s regulations establish Nursing Officer as a Group B, non-ministerial cadre post.
Clinical responsibilities are performed within a nursing chain of supervision and in coordination with medical and allied-health teams.

sections.heading: Progression and further study
paragraphs: A typical supervisory progression in the AIIMS New Delhi nursing structure is Nursing Officer, Senior Nursing Officer, Assistant Nursing Superintendent, Deputy Nursing Superintendent, Nursing Superintendent, and Chief Nursing Officer. This is an institutional hierarchy, not a guarantee of promotion: eligibility, seniority, performance, vacancy position, service rules, and departmental selection procedures are employer-specific.

Further education can support clinical specialization, education, administration, research, and public-health career options. The Indian Nursing Council lists M.Sc. Nursing eligibility as requiring Nurse and Midwife (or equivalent) registration, an eligible B.Sc. Nursing qualification with at least 55% aggregate marks, and the stated one-year experience requirement; candidates should confirm the current regulations and individual university rules before applying.
bullets: Pursue internal promotion or departmental selection routes where service rules permit.
Build supervisory and ward-management capability for Senior Nursing Officer and nursing-administration pathways.
Consider M.Sc. Nursing for advanced nursing education and specialization; it does not automatically confer a government promotion.

Consider allied higher-study routes such as MPH or MBA where they align with an intended shift toward public health, management, quality, or administration, subject to each programme’s admissions rules.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: Compensation should be read from the recruitment notice and service rules for the specific employer and posting city. As dated central-government reference points, ESIC’s 2020 regulations list Nursing Officer as Group B, non-ministerial at 7th CPC Pay Level 7, with a published pay-matrix range of ₹44,900–₹1,42,400. AIIMS NORCET-8, advertised in February 2025, also described Nursing Officer posts at Level 7.

The amount ultimately received can differ from the pay-matrix figure because applicable allowances, deductions, city of posting, employment terms, and subsequent government revisions may vary. The AIIMS NORCET-8 notice stated that the posts carried the usual allowances admissible to Central Government servants of similar status at the respective city or institute.
bullets: Use Pay Level and the employer’s current notification as the primary comparison point rather than relying on generic salary estimates.
Check whether the notice specifies Central or state pay rules, the appointment type, probation conditions, posting location, and allowances.
Treat the cited ₹44,900–₹1,42,400 Level 7 range as ESIC’s 2020 regulatory context, not a guaranteed current take-home amount for every government Nursing Officer post.
